sections.title: Definite Articles (el, la, los, las)
sections.explanation: Definite articles in Spanish agree with the gender and number of the noun. Masculine singular: el, feminine singular: la, masculine plural: los, feminine plural: las. They correspond to English 'the'. Special rule: use 'el' (not 'la') before feminine nouns that begin with a stressed 'a' or 'ha' sound — e.g., 'el agua' (water), 'el área' (area). This is purely for ease of pronunciation; the noun is still feminine, so adjectives remain feminine: 'el agua fría'.
sections.tip: el (m. sg.) | la (f. sg.) | los (m. pl.) | las (f. pl.) — Special: el agua, el área (stressed a-)

examples.es: El libro está en la mesa.
examples.en: The book is on the table.

examples.es: Los niños juegan en el parque.
examples.en: The children play in the park.

examples.es: El área del proyecto es grande.

exercises.prompt: Choose the correct definite article: '___ perro ladra.' (dog — masculine singular)
exercises.explanation: 'Perro' (dog) is masculine singular, so the definite article is 'el'.

exercises.prompt: Choose the correct definite article: '___ ciudad es hermosa.' (city — feminine singular)
exercises.explanation: 'Ciudad' (city) is feminine singular, so the definite article is 'la'.

exercises.prompt: Which is correct for 'agua' (water, feminine noun with stressed a-)?
exercises.explanation: Feminine nouns beginning with a stressed 'a' use 'el' in the singular to avoid the awkward 'la a-' sound. 'Agua' is still feminine: 'el agua fría'.

exercises.prompt: Find and fix the error: 'La área de la cocina es pequeña.'
exercises.incorrectText: La área de la cocina es pequeña.
exercises.correctedText: El área de la cocina es pequeña.
acceptedAnswers: El área de la cocina es pequeña.
El área de la cocina es pequeña
exercises.explanation: 'Área' begins with a stressed 'a', so despite being feminine it takes 'el' in the singular: 'el área'.

exercises.prompt: Match each noun with its correct definite article:
exercises.explanation: el = masculine singular, la = feminine singular, los = masculine plural, las = feminine plural.

exercises.prompt: Me gusta ___ música clásica. (music — feminine singular)
exercises.explanation: 'Música' is feminine singular, so use 'la': 'Me gusta la música clásica.'
'Hombres' (men) is masculine plural, so the correct article is 'los'.
'Los niños corren en el parque.' — The children run in the park.

sections.title: Indefinite Articles (un, una, unos, unas)
sections.explanation: Indefinite articles introduce a noun for the first time or refer to a non-specific thing. Masculine singular: un, feminine singular: una, masculine plural: unos, feminine plural: unas. They correspond to English 'a/an' (singular) and 'some' (plural). Important: omit the indefinite article after the verb 'ser' when stating an unmodified profession, nationality, or religion — 'Soy médico' (I am a doctor). But if you add an adjective, the article returns: 'Soy un médico excelente'.
sections.tip: un (m. sg.) | una (f. sg.) | unos (m. pl.) | unas (f. pl.) — Omit after ser with professions: Soy médico

examples.en: I have a cat at home.

examples.es: Hay unos libros en la estantería.
examples.en: There are some books on the shelf.
I am a teacher. (no article — profession after ser)

exercises.prompt: Choose the correct indefinite article: 'Quiero ___ café, por favor.' (coffee — masculine singular)
exercises.explanation: 'Café' (coffee) is masculine singular, so the indefinite article is 'un'.

exercises.prompt: Hay ___ naranja en la nevera. (orange — feminine singular)
Which sentence correctly omits the article?
exercises.explanation: After 'ser', unmodified professions drop the indefinite article: 'Mi padre es ingeniero.' (My father is an engineer.)

exercises.prompt: When is the article required with a profession?
exercises.explanation: When an adjective modifies the profession, the indefinite article is required: 'Ella es una doctora brillante.' (She is a brilliant doctor.)

exercises.prompt: Vi ___ películas interesantes esta semana. (movies — feminine plural)
exercises.explanation: 'Películas' (movies) is feminine plural, so use 'unas': 'Vi unas películas interesantes.'

exercises.prompt: Find and fix the error: 'Ella es una abogada.' (She is a lawyer — no adjective)
exercises.explanation: When stating a profession without an adjective after 'ser', drop the article: 'Ella es abogada.'

exercises.prompt: Match each noun with its correct indefinite article:
exercises.explanation: un = masculine singular, una = feminine singular, unos = masculine plural, unas = feminine plural.

exercises.prompt: Necesito ___ bolígrafo para escribir. (pen — masculine singular)

exercises.explanation: 'Madrid es una ciudad bonita.' — Madrid is a beautiful city. 'Ciudad' is feminine, so 'una'.
